content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

June 13, 1977

Ted L. Smith
Chief Probation Officer
Placer County
360 Elm Avenue
Auburn, California 95603

Dear Mr. Smith:

Thank you for your recent letter in which you
suggested there is no valid reason for filing a 16
page Statement of Economic Interest each year if
there is no change in any of the reportable interests.
The Commission is in absolute agreement.

Enclosed is a copy of a short form which the
Commission recommends to you. Please note No. 7 which
accomplishes the purpose of full disclosure with a
minimum amount of paperwork.

Please feel free to call on me if you have any
questions.

Sincerely,

Robert M. Stern
General Counsel

RMS:nc

Enclosure

May 6, 1977


Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95814

Gentlemen:

In accord with Government Code Sections 87300, etc., it is necessary for several persons employed in the Placer County Probation Department to file conflict of interest statements on an annual basis. We have no problem in the initial filing; however, it currently is necessary to file a 16-page annual report which is usually a copy of the initial effort. It would be much easier and a lot less expensive and time consuming if a summary sheet were available for subsequent filings when there is no change in the economic interest of the employee.

I am sure this concern is not solely mine, but is shared by many persons who have to file financial statements.

The first page of the "Local Agency Statement of Economic Interests" form could suffice as an annual report by adding one additional statement on its face. That statement could read, "There is no change from the annual statement filed on February ____, 19 ____."

Such a move would certainly be cost effective.

Thank you for considering my thoughts.

Sincerely,

TED L. SMITH
Chief Probation Officer

TLS/bs
